question.text: Under Section 23JC of SCRA, when is a legal representative of a deceased person liable to pay a penalty?

answer.text: In all cases regardless of when the penalty was imposed
Only if the penalty was imposed within 1 year of death
Only if the penalty was imposed before the death of the deceased person
Legal representatives are never liable for penalties

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 23JC states that the legal representative is liable only in case the penalty is imposed before the death of the deceased person. **The Trap:** The first option suggesting liability in all cases seems harsh but could be tempting if one doesn't read the specific limitation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Liability provisions are tested across multiple NISM certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This provision ensures that penalties imposed during a person's lifetime survive their death, limited to the estate.
